findings: Identity, scientific name, family, measurements, habitat, behavior, and field-mark text agree with the verified sources. The main specimen correctly shows one head, one bill, two legs with feet, one long rounded dark tail, and a side-view wing; the separate head and wing are intentional study details, not duplicate anatomy.

Plumage and proportions match the supplied references: slate-gray body, darker cap, narrow straight dark bill, dark eye, broad rounded wing, long dark legs, blackish rounded tail, and rufous/chestnut undertail patch.
No place name, ZIP code, coordinates, map, or local-observation detail appears.

Concrete issue: the labeled 0–24 cm ruler implies the main bird is only about 15 cm from bill tip to tail tip, conflicting with the printed and verified 21–24 cm length. Treat the ruler as decorative or rescale/remove it for scientific precision.

verification_sources.title: Gray Catbird Identification — All About Birds
